title: Question 1 (Question 5.2 in Fujiwara–Papasoglu)
statement_text: Is there a constant $k$ such that for any graph $H$, the class of $H$-minor free graphs has asymptotic dimension at most $k$? Can we take $k = 2$?
context_text: Fujiwara and Papasoglu proved that the class of planar graphs has asymptotic dimension at most 3 and asked whether there exists a uniform upper bound on the asymptotic dimension of proper minor-closed families. Ostrovskii and Rosenthal had earlier proved that for every integer $t$, the class of $K_t$-minor free graphs has asymptotic dimension at most $4t$; this question is posed as a possible common strengthening of both results.
paper_contribution: Proves that for any graph $H$ the class of $H$-minor free graphs has asymptotic dimension at most 2 (Theorem 1.1), giving a complete and optimal solution to both parts of the question.
notes: The paper text is truncated; additional conjectures or questions appearing later in the manuscript may not be captured here.
paper_title: Asymptotic Dimension of Minor-Closed Families and Assouad-Nagata Dimension of Surfaces
